description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a double-wing structure, featuring a blue upper wing and a green lower wing, both supported by yellow struts and black rigging wires. The fuselage is streamlined and painted in a muted blue, with a single open cockpit positioned towards the front. The tail assembly is painted yellow and consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The nose houses a visible piston engine with a two-blade propeller, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, with exposed structural elements and a lightweight appearance.
description: A vintage blue and green biplane with yellow tail and struts, open cockpit, and a front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue upper wing, green lower wing, yellow tail, and the exposed engine with propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the yellow tail fin, dual horizontal stabilizers, and the full span of the blue and green wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the wing structure, struts, and engine details.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the two-blade propeller, open cockpit, and the biplane's stacked wing configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts and rigging wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit integrated into the front section of the fuselage.
